scenario_title: add_rr at a CNAME owner with no simultaneous delete
test_rationale: This test isolates the gap by starting with a clean CNAME owner and then issuing a single add_rr for a different type at the same name, with no prerequisite or delete to remove the CNAME. Standards require CNAME exclusivity, but do not define whether the UPDATE should be rejected with a name-conflict style error or accepted by replacing/augmenting the RRset. The follow-up queries make both the reply and the post-update zone state observable.

group_1.behavior: Reject the UPDATE because the target name already owns a CNAME and the add_rr would violate CNAME exclusivity; preserve the original CNAME-only state.
group_1.post_state: alias.campus.edu. continues to have only the CNAME RRset; no A RRset is present.

group_2.behavior: Accept or transform the UPDATE in a way that permits the new RR to coexist with or replace the existing CNAME.
group_2.post_state: alias.campus.edu. has an A RRset and either the CNAME is removed or an inconsistent dual-RR state is observable.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2181 §10.1 says a CNAME owner may have no other data, but RFC 2136 §3.4.3 does not specify how an UPDATE processor must respond when an add_rr targets a name that already owns a CNAME. That leaves both the update RCODE and the resulting zone state underspecified.
